message:
br-bd-haq43 EXT4_IOC_CLEAR_ES_CACHE ioctl: drop ext4 extent status cache

ext4_clear_inode_es (fs/ext4/ioctl.c) drops the in-memory extent
status (es) cache for an inode so the next read repopulates it
from the on-disk extent tree. e2fsprogs uses
EXT4_IOC_CLEAR_ES_CACHE = _IO('f', 40) = 0x6628 to defeat caching
after offline metadata edits via debugfs. Pre-fix dispatch_ioctl
returned ENOTTY because the FUSE handler had no branch.

ffs-core surface:
- New `FsOps::clear_extent_status_cache(cx, scope, ino) -> Result<()>`
  trait method (default Ok(()) — best-effort hint).
- OpenFs override: ext4 reads the inode through ext4_canonical_inode
  to surface ENOENT/EINVAL on bogus inode numbers, then returns Ok
  because FrankenFS keeps extent state per-RequestScope rather than
  in a process-lifetime per-inode cache (there is nothing to drop).
  btrfs runs the equivalent inode-existence check via
  btrfs_read_inode_attr (it has no es-cache analogue; FIEMAP walks
  extent_data items inline each time).

ffs-fuse wiring:
- EXT4_IOC_CLEAR_ES_CACHE = 0x0000_6628 (no input/output payload;
  it's _IO with size=0).
- dispatch_ioctl branch ignores in_data + out_size per _IO contract,
  routes through `ops.clear_extent_status_cache(...)` under
  RequestOp::IoctlRead scope, and returns IoctlResult::Data(empty)
  on success or IoctlResult::Error(errno) on backend failure.
- IoctlRecordingFs gains an IoctlCall::ClearEsCache(InodeNumber)
  trace variant + matching `clear_extent_status_cache` impl.

Tests: 708 ffs-core + 287 ffs-fuse pass. New regressions:
- dispatch_ioctl_clear_es_cache_routes_to_fsops_with_empty_reply:
  pins success path, asserts Begin(IoctlRead) + ClearEsCache(ino)
  + End(IoctlRead) trace.
- dispatch_ioctl_clear_es_cache_ignores_input_payload_and_out_size:
  proves dispatcher mirrors _IO semantics (any in_data length /
  out_size accepted, never read).

Refs: bd-haq43

files.patch:
@@ -1066,6 +1066,26 @@ pub trait FsOps: Send + Sync {
         Ok(())
     }
 
+    /// Drop the in-memory extent status cache for an inode.
+    ///
+    /// Surfaces what `EXT4_IOC_CLEAR_ES_CACHE` triggers in the kernel:
+    /// `fs/ext4/ioctl.c::ext4_clear_inode_es` removes every cached
+    /// `ext4_es_status` entry so the next read repopulates state from
+    /// the on-disk extent tree. e2fsprogs uses this to defeat caching
+    /// after offline metadata edits via `debugfs`. FrankenFS keeps
+    /// extent state per-`RequestScope` rather than in a long-lived
+    /// inode-level cache, so the default implementation is a successful
+    /// no-op — backends only need to override if they expose a
+    /// process-lifetime cache that needs explicit invalidation.
+    fn clear_extent_status_cache(
+        &self,
+        _cx: &Cx,
+        _scope: &mut RequestScope,
+        _ino: InodeNumber,
+    ) -> ffs_error::Result<()> {
+        Ok(())
+    }
+
     /// Get ext4 inode generation (`EXT4_IOC_GETVERSION`).
     ///
     /// Returns the raw `i_generation` field for the given inode.
